item.title: Overthrown Review
item.content:encoded:
Overthrown is pitched as a chaotic city-builder. Like a Minecraft mash-up with Kingdom Two Crowns, the player must explore the world and collect resources to build and develop their kingdom, all whilst protecting their home from those who want to destroy it and steal the crown. Despite this simple pitch, Overthrown is much more than just a city-builder; it’s also an exploration game, a combat game, a defence game and a survival game. As you’d expect, this is a lot of genre parts to stitch together, and the resulting product is a bit of a Franken-game. Some sections fuse together nicely, where others stick out like an oversized wolf-arm stitched onto a schoolgirl. On the plus side, the variety of gameplay elements fully fleshes out the bones of the game – unlike my Frankenstein’s monster metaphor which has definitely been stretched too thin now.

Throughout Overthrown, the player researches new innovations to unlock new structures, buildings and features for the kingdom – of which there are multitudes! The city-builder elements to the game are well stacked with options and the possibilities are endless. Even the landscapes can be adapted block by block to suit the player’s needs. This is a great chance to get creative, play around and make a unique and distinctive kingdom.

On the downside, the actual gameplay in Overthrown can be a little fussy and frustrating. A few quality-of-life features fall short, leaving you feeling obstructed and exasperated. As you progress through the research tree to unlock new village structures, the selection wheel in build mode becomes overwhelmingly crowded and tricky to use. Moreover, the game doesn’t keep a tally of which buildings you’ve constructed or how many. Because of this – and the sheer quantity of buildings available – it’s easy to lose track of what they’ve recently unlocked and whether they’ve already constructed it.

Further to this, Overthrown provides next to no tutorials. Although this might be expected – or even enjoyable – for players who love the exploration and creative side of the game, the lack of guidance can make the town-builder elements of the gameplay cryptic and confusing. There’s little to no detail given on how the buildings, world elements and villagers interact, and inadequate time given to the player to experiment and work it out. The resources you need to unlock a building or make it function are shown to you as miniscule indefinable icons. Is that a flame? A sack? A piece of fluff? Oh no, that’s not a resource icon at all, it’s just a mark on my screen. As the in-game days tick by, the player is bombarded with urgent but bewildering messages on everything that’s going wrong with the village but with no explanation as to how to resolve it. Chaotic is certainly an apt description.

The wider world itself is enjoyable to explore; skim across the surface of a lake at top speed, pit beasts against outlaws to eliminate all your foes in one fell swoop, harvest stacks of resources all at once by dashing right through them. It’s a shame then, that the map features are so restrictive. Primarily, it’s impossible to tell which way your character is facing on the map. There was more than one occasion where I found myself speeding off into the wilderness, only to realise I’d gone completely the wrong way. What’s more, the aforementioned miniscule indefinable resource icons only appear in the area surrounding your character. Perhaps there’s more of what you need on the other side of the kingdom? Who knows! You’ll have to make your way over there and keep checking your map every few seconds to see if what you need turns up. That’s if you can tell which icon is which.

All in all, Overthrown has the potential to have a little bit of something for everyone. Perhaps with a few more improvements now that it’s reached a 1.0 release, and a jolt or two from an electrode – sorry, there’s that Frankenstein metaphor again – it could fulfil that potential.

Summary

Overthrown is an ambitious genre melting-pot of a game that offers a monster-mash of potential, but its cluttered systems and lack of guidance ultimately hold it back from fully realising its promise.

Good

  • Wide variety of structures to unlock
  • Freedom of creativity
  • A little bit of everything in the genre melting pot

Bad

  • Crowded construction wheel
  • Lack of tutorials limit accessibility
  • Stunted map function

Related stories from TSA



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/02/overthrown-review/

item.title: NYT Mini Crossword Hints Today—Answers For Thursday, April 2
item.content:encoded:
The NYT Mini Crossword is a popular, quick-to-complete word game to test yourself daily, but some of the puzzles can be difficult to complete, so here are some hints.

If you’ve found yourself stuck on today’s NYT Mini, Insider Gaming has some helpful hints and all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on Thursday, April 2, 2026.

IMPORTANT: The following information includes spoilers on today’s edition of the NYT Mini Crossword. If you don’t want the answer given to you, stop scrolling.

How to Play NYT Mini Crossword

The NYT Mini Crossword is available to play on the New York Times Games app or website, but, as of September 2025, a New York Times Game subscription is required.

The NYT Games Subscription is priced at $6.95 per month, but there are introductory offers available for other NYT subscriptions that include games access, which might be worth considering.

Without a Games subscription, access to the NYT Mini Crossword is blocked.

NYT Mini Crossword Clues (April 2)

Here are all the clues provided for the NYT Mini Crossword for April 2, 2026.

Across

1 – Man’s name that’s Italian for ‘eight’

5 – Part of the Statue of Liberty that’s accessed by a double-helix staircase

6 – See eye to eye

7 – Spiked clubs used by knights

8 – Sharply clear, as a TV broadcast

Down

1 – Instrument in a baseball stadium

2 – Part of the Statue of Liberty that was once open to the public, but closed in 1916

3 – Coarse fabric for some suit jackets

4 – Column to the right of the tens

5 – Women’s sleeveless undershirt, for short

Additional Clues For NYT Mini (April 2)

Here are some additional clues to help you solve the NYT Mini Crossword on April 2, 2026.

Across

1 – 2022 film starring Tom Hanks, ‘A Man Called ____’

5 – Headpiece for a monarch

6 – Consent to a proposal

7 – Designed to crush armoured opponents

8 – High Definition

Down

1 – Like the heart or lungs

2 – Marvel hero, ‘The Human ____’

3 – Traditionally handwoven in Scotland

4 – ‘These shoes are uncomfortable, I need new ____’

5 – Like a tank top

All NYT Mini Crossword Answers (April 2)

Here are all the answers for the NYT Mini Crossword on April 2, 2026.

Across

1 – OTTO

5 – CROWN

6 – AGREE

7 – MACES

8 – INHD

Down

1 – ORGAN

2 – TORCH

3 – TWEED

4 – ONES

5 – CAMI

When is The Next Game?

The NYT Mini Crossword refreshes daily at 10 PM ET on weekdays and Saturdays, and at 6 PM ET on Sundays.

As such, the next NYT Mini Crossword will be available at 7 PM PT/9 PM CT/10 PM ET/3 AM GMT/2 PM AET.

What is The NYT Mini Crossword?

The NYT Mini Crossword was introduced in 2014 as a shorter and quicker-to-complete game to accompany the main New York Times Crossword, which was initially released in 1942.

Until September 2025, the NYT Mini Crossword was free, but it has now been consolidated into a monthly NYT Games Subscription alongside several other popular games.

Did you struggle with the NYT Mini Crossword today? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

item.title: Goat Simulator maker Coffee Stain shutters Malmo studio
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

April 2, 2026

1 Min Read

Logo and screenshot via Coffee Stain

Swedish mobile studio Coffee Stain Malmo has been shuttered. Coffee Stain Publishing's vice president of mobile Daniel Persson shared the news on LinkedIn and explained their role has been eliminated following the closure. 

"Building this office from the ground up has been one of the most rewarding experiences of my career. It started as an idea, that grew into an amazing team of talented people. I couldn't be more proud of what we have accomplished together," he wrote. 

"To every single person who was part of this journey: Thank you. Your skill, dedication, and creativity made this place what it was. I wholeheartedly recommend each and every one of you. Any team lucky enough to bring you on board will be better for it."

According to the studio website, Coffee Stain Malmo comprised 17 employees. It's currently unclear whether the entire team has been laid off as a result of the shutdown. 

Coffee Stain Malmo worked on a number of notable mobile titles including Goat Simulator 3 and Songs of Conquest Mobile. The studio is a subsidiary of Coffee Stain Publishing, which was spun off from Swedish conglomerate Embracer Group in 2025. 

Related:Call of Duty: Black Ops 7 appears to earn an estimated $30,000 on Steam per negative review

The move came after Embracer confirmed it would be splitting its business into three standalone entities to search for "winning formulas" after embarking upon a restructuring program that resulted in widespread layoffs, project cancellations, studio closures, and divestment. 

The layoffs, however, have continued, with Embracer cutting roles at both Crystal Dynamics and Eidos Montreal in recent weeks. 

Game Developer has reached out to Coffee Stain Publishing for comment.

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/goat-simulator-maker-coffee-stain-shutters-malmo-studio

item.title: Original Resident Evil trilogy with GOG's fresh coat of paint is finally on Steam, but you will have to put up with Enigma DRM
item.content:encoded:
Retro Capcom RPG Breath of Fire 4's also arrived on the Steamy storefront in this mass migration

Wake up, the Residents are Evil again. Well, what I mean to say is that the oldest Evil Residents, the most classic of Resident Evils, have now hit Steam in the nicely revamped form they took on when GOG brought them back to PC a couple of years ago. Yep, the versions of Resis 1, 2, and 3 that've just hit Valve's storefront include GOG's handy tweaks to help them run in all singing and all dancing fashion on modern hardware.

Steam listings for these GOG-co developed versions of 1996's Resident Evil, 1998's Resident Evil 2, and 1999's Resident Evil 3 Nemesis have all popped up today, April 2nd. Joining them in making the jump from GOG is classic RPG Breath of Fire IV.

As of writing, they're all on sale at 50% off, meaning you can grab each of them for £4/$5/€5 until 6PM BST/1PM ET/10AM PDT on April 15th. For your money, you get versions of the games featuring a bunch of handy tweaks and additions GOG have made to make them easier or better to play on today's computers. The original Resi, for example, comes with the following:

  • All 4 localisations of the game included (English, German, French, Japanese)
  • Improved DirectX game renderer
  • New rendering options (Windowed Mode, Vertical Synchronization Control, Gamma Correction, Integer Scaling, Anti-Aliasing and more)
  • Improved timing of the cutscenes
  • Improved game video player
  • Improved game registry settings
  • Issue-free game exit and task switching

They reportedly work pretty fine on Steam Deck too, provided you're willing to set up custom inputs or use some that the community have set up.

There is one difference compared to the versions on GOG itself, though. All four games' Steam iterations come with third party Enigma DRM. That'd be the same make of DRM which Capcom recently pulled from the Resident Evil 4 Remake, with Digital Foundry reporting that the anti-piracy software was impacting the game's performance.

If you want an idea of just how foundational an experience playing these classic Resis can be, I direct you to Dave Irwin (RPS in peace)'s recollection of turning off the first one multiple times as a ten year old. It's ok Dave, DRM scares me too.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/original-resident-evil-trilogy-with-gogs-fresh-coat-of-paint-is-finally-on-steam-but-you-will-have-to-put-up-with-enigma-drm

item.title: CS2 Animgraph 2 Beta Launched as Valve Eyes Animation Overall
item.content:encoded:
The April 1 Counter-Strike 2 update saw Valve launch its public Beta test for Animgraph 2 animations. Despite the date, it is no April Fools, but a rehaul of CS2 animations that has been promised for months.

What is Animgraph 2?

Animgraph 2 is the next generation of Valve’s animation system, replacing the original AnimGraph model used in prior titles on the Source 2 engine.

Counter-Strike was originally released fully on AnimGraph, but has since had components updated to Animgraph 2 as CS2 gradually migrates over.

In July 28 2025, all first-person animations, including weapon deploy, firing, reload, and inspect animations were migrated over to Animgraph 2. Additionally, the in-game chickens also utilized Animgraph, a zero-consequence way of testing the build.

Now Valve seems to have enough confidence to put the system into more impactful parts of the game, testing the third-peron animations which players rely on for knowledge of where to aim, and if their bullets hit anything.

The system aims to create a snappier system for players by reducing CPU and networking costs associated with animation. Since CS2’s launch, the delay between hitting a headshot and getting feedback has been a sore spot for the FPS.

Valve is also attempting to make the animations more reliable for players, announcing adjustments to player height on sloped surfaces and in-air crouching animations.

How to access the CS2 Animgraph 2 Beta

Players will need to opt in to the CS2 Animgraphy 2 Beta in order to join in the testing.

To opt in to the CS2 Animgraph 2 Beta

  1. Go to your Steam Library
  2. Right-click on CS2 and select Properties
  3. Go to Game Versions & Betas
  4. Select ‘animgraph_2_beta’ from the list
  5. The game will then update to the Beta version, and you can play.

No online multiplayer modes are available when testing the Beta, so have fun shooting bots!

You can discuss Counter-Strike 2’s direction on our Discord server.
For other CS2 news, see how FaZe’s Cologne Major chances are still alive.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

item.title: Today’s Contexto: Hints & Answer For April 2, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Contexto is a word association game that gives you a new word to guess every single day. With every attempt, the AI algorithm determines how close your guess is to the daily answer based on thematic and contextual similarity.

Sometimes, though, no matter how experienced you are, the word you’re looking for can be incredibly vague and hard to deduce. Luckily for you, this guide is full of hints to give you a helping hand, but make sure you give today’s Contexto a go if you haven’t already!

How To Play Contexto

Contexto is incredibly simple to play, but difficult to master. All you have to do is type words into the search bar, and the AI algorithm will tell you how close your word is to the answer.

You’ll know how close your guess is based on the colour of the bar and the number next to it.

  • Red includes guesses with the number 1,501 and above, meaning you’re way off from the secret word.
  • Orange includes guesses between 301 and 1,500, meaning you’re getting closer, but still some ways off.
  • Green includes guesses between 1 and 300, meaning you’re on the right track.

Best Contexto Tips & Tricks

  • Start off using broad terms, then narrow down once you’ve got under the 1,000 mark.
  • Try using Nouns first, and dip into verbs or adjectives once you’ve discovered the context.
  • Experiment with random words at times, especially as different forms of a root word can rank differently, such as work and worker.
  • Guess away, as unlike other word games like Wordle, you aren’t limited to a strict number of attempts.

Best Starting Words For Contexto

Here are some great starting words to get you going with Contexto:

  • Thing
  • Person
  • Place
  • Animal
  • Work
  • Nature
  • Game
  • Concept
  • Idea
  • Food

Contexto Hints For Today, April 2, 2026

Without giving away today’s Contexto answer, here are a few helpful hints to get you thinking in the right direction:

  • Hint 1: Mythical creature
  • Hint 2: Often breathes fire
  • Hint 3: Dungeons & _______

If they don’t help, then here are the nine closest words, going from tenth to second:

  • Dungeon
  • Quest
  • Knight
  • Sword
  • Creature
  • Beast
  • Warrior
  • Demon
  • Monster

Today’s Contexto Answer For April 2, 2026

If you’ve tried everything and still can’t figure out today’s Contexto answer, then you can find it down below:

Answer: Dragon

A Dragon is a large, winged serpent, often represented with a long tail and fire coming out of its mouth.

When Is The Next Contexto Game?

Contexto is updated daily at 12 AM or Midnight in your respective time zone, meaning the next game will start on April 3.

The Contexto schedule is very consistent, with a new game releasing at the same time every day.

How did you find today’s Contexto? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more word game guides, check out the following:

  • NYT Connections

item.title: JD Actor Suggests The Coalition Looked at Gears 6 Before Going with E-Day
item.content:encoded:
A new Gears of War game will be released later this year, seven years after Gears 5, but it won't be Gears 6. Instead of ending the series' second trilogy, Vancouver-based developer The Coalition chose to create a prequel called Gears of War: E-Day, which centers on the very first day the Locust Horde emerged from the planet's underground to overwhelm the surface of Sera. According to the studio, this will be their most ambitious game yet.

Earlier this week, Microsoft announced that this year's Xbox Game Showcase, due to air on Sunday, June 7 at 10 AM Pacific Time, will be followed by a Gears of War E-Day Direct featuring new details, gameplay, and insights about the next installment in the third-person shooter franchise.

Australian actor Liam McIntyre, who played JD Fenix in Gears of War 4 and Gears 5, responded enthusiastically to the news on his X account, prompting some of his followers to inquire about the state of Gears 6. McIntyre then revealed that it had been hinted at, but The Coalition later changed their minds and selected the E-Day project instead; he still hopes the game will be developed after E-Day.

We did Gears 4 and 5, and there was a suggestion that maybe there would be a Gears 6. But the team wanted to try something new, as I understand it. And honestly, it looks great. Can't wait to play.

I hope they go back to it, but I love those guys and just want them to make a game they feel nails the Gears experience everyone loves. That'd be awesome. And hey, if that leads back to the trilogy, happy days.

A follower also shared the hope that the character of JD Fenix would turn up alive in a potential Gears 6 (towards the end of Gears 5, players can choose whether to save JD or Del), and McIntyre provided his own plot solution to the conundrum:

I have a solve for that regardless of what you choose. Hoping that Del is canon, Marcus loses his mind and goes rogue, and you learn JD has kind of been revived and controlled by the Swarm, his dad has to fight him. But somehow you save him. Not sure how yet.

McIntyre, best known for his role as Spartacus in the Starz television series Spartacus: Vengeance, will also be featured later this year in Insomniac's Marvel's Wolverine as the protagonist, James "Logan" Howlett (Wolverine).

As for Gears 6, given that it took seven years to make E-Day, a new installment in the trilogy would likely launch well beyond 2030. Best not think about it too much at this point.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/the-coalition-looked-gears-6-before-e-day/

item.title: The sequel to Garry's Mod releases this month, and Valve will let you publish your creations as standalone Steam games, like dafty Finn sim My Summer
item.content:encoded:
Garry Newman and Facepunch announce new license deal

S&box is an upcoming open source game development platform, built using a modified Source 2 engine. Out on 28th April, it's the work of the very same Garry Newman behind the legendary Garry's Mod, who's been working on a spiritual successor at his company Facepunch Studios (also the developers of Rust, and originally known as Team Garry) since 2015.

Inasmuch as I am qualified to judge, S&box sounds like a robust tool – it's got features and systems for visual scripting, open world terrain, and creating shaders. The developers are also supporting VR, but hey, nobody's perfect. The latest, bestest news is that Facepunch have just struck a deal with Valve to let people export their S&box creations as standalone Steam games, without paying Facepunch a fee.

"We signed the new license with Valve this week, allowing us to allow people to export games from s&box's editor and ship them as standalone games on Steam without paying us anything," Newman writes on the project's blog, via Gaming On Linux. "This has been a bit of a complicated journey, but with a lot of reassurance and compromises, we've got there.

"We still have work to do on our end," he goes on. "We need to create a license between Facepunch and the people shipping games, then double and triple check everything is legit, but I wanted to share this progress with the community so they know that Valve did the bizzo."

"When it's ready we'll be piloting it with a few select people," Newman concludes. "The first out the door is likely to be My Summer Cottage."

What is My Summer Cottage? It appears to be the house equivalent of My Summer Car - the "ultimate Finnish life simulator", in which 1-to-4 players fish, hunt, forage, go to saunas, play slot machines, hurl bottles, and generally disgrace themselves.

"MY SUMMER COTTAGE is powered by s&box, a game engine based on Source 2!" the game's developers Small Fish write on their Steam page. "We kind of weren't able to confirm this officially before, but now that the agreement is almost done, we can finally confirm that this game is running on the s&box engine."

You can read more about s&box on the public development roadmap. Will this be the hit Garry's Mod was? Well, there are a lot of game creation tools in circulation these days, but the Gmod association and the seeming simplicity of publishing on Steam should earn it a few devotees. You can find out more on the s&box Steam page. Oh! I've just got the pun.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/the-sequel-to-garrys-mod-releases-this-month-and-valve-will-let-you-publish-your-creations-as-standalone-steam-games-like-dafty-finn-sim-my-summer-cottage

item.title: ARC Raiders Devs Fixed One of the Game’s Biggest Problems
item.content:encoded:
Embark Studios recently released the Flashpoint update for ARC Raiders, the electrifying extraction title that has now been around for more than five months. The update adds new ARC threats, a couple of weapons, a new event, and some other bits and pieces, just to keep that roadmap juicy enough to retain players (kind of).

However, fans are less concerned about the new content and more enthused by the idea of Embark Studios finally fixing one of the game’s biggest problems. It’s one of the highlights of the patch, and it has fans celebrating the effort put in by Embark to listen to the community.

Late Spawns Being Cut Down

In the patch notes for the Flashpoint update, many have overlooked a tidbit from Embark Studios that’s wonderful news for fans of ARC Raiders. The game has been around for almost half a year, and while there’s plenty of road left ahead, the team is trying fairly hard to please the community to ensure the players stick with the title while said roadmap unravels.

At the top of the newly released patch notes for the latest ARC Raiders update, it reads:

Players who build their own loadout are more likely to join fresh servers.

Since ARC Raiders came out, this has been a massive point of contention for players.

When you load into a raid in ARC Raiders, you might wind up entering the map with the game timer halved. What should be a 30-minute raid might only be 15 or 20 minutes for you, and that’s a huge issue when you consider the size of some of the game’s larger maps.

It also means that you could spend time building a sweet kit just to have a slashed raid timer, coming face to face with players who have already spent up to 10 or 15 minutes in their raid, plucking the best loot out of the place. That’s before we even start talking about the lack of time to complete objectives, trials, feats, and so on…

It’s a balancing mechanic, which is something Embark explained a while ago. To keep the lobby fresh and stocked with players, Embark Studios feeds some users in later than others. It’s the same way ‘player Scav’ characters work in Escape from Tarkov, loading in late to keep the lobby feeling alive.

This latest change in ARC Raiders means that, if you spend the time and in-game money building a kit, you’ll be more likely to join a server with a full clock.

We’ve not tested this, so it’s hard to judge just how likely ‘more likely‘ is.

Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord server if you’ve noticed this change taking effect in your games.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Stormgate is going offline

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Stormgate Going Offline as Provider Hit by AI Pivot
item.content:encoded:
Stormgate, once penned as the most promising real-time strategy game in years and dubbed a massive esports prospect, is going offline at the end of April. It’s being taken off Steam because of a tricky situation with the game’s multiplayer provider, Hathora. The team at Frost Giant Studios shared the unfortunate news recently on Discord.

It was explained that Hathora has been bought out by an AI company that is expected to refocus resources in a grand pivot to LLM development. The only way Stormgate will retain online functionality is if a suitable partner is found, but for now, the game’s servers will be shut down in just a few weeks.

Stormgate Offline Mode Incoming?

Frost Giant Studios has stressed the intention to add an offline mode to Stormgate, the RTS title created by former Blizzard developers that surfaced in 2022. The game landed on Steam in 2025, almost two years after it entered a closed beta.

As a free-to-play title in a tight niche, Stormgate never made immense waves in the gaming space, but it was good enough to draw interest from those experienced in games like StarCraft.

In a recent message on the game’s Discord server, the team at Frost Giant Studios shared the dire news:

We have some unfortunate news. I’ll get straight to it:

  • Our game server orchestration partner, Hathora, has been purchased by an AI company, and they are winding down their service at the end of April. This will create a planned outage for Stormgate’s multiplayer modes.
  • Stormgate will be patched so that it can be played offline, but online modes will not be available at that point.
  • We hope to restore online play in a future patch, but this work will be dependent on Frost Giant finding a partner to support ongoing operations.

We’re very grateful to our community of players, and we will post another update as more information is available, including more info about how offline mode works, and whether we get any patches out before the server wind down. Thank you for your support, The Frost Giant and Community Development team.

(Thanks to delistedgames.com for the spot)

On Steam, the game’s review sentiments have plummeted, marking it as ‘Mostly Negative’ in response to the news. Those who purchased the game more recently are hopeful that Frost Giant Studios will extend some kind of refund policy.

Stormgate started life as a crowdfunded title that boasted immense esports potential, especially given the decreasing energy in the competitive RTS space. It managed to pull in a massive $2.3 million from backers, 30 of whom stumped up a whopping $5,000 to support the development of the title.

Are you shocked at the imminent shutdown of Stormgate? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Nexon has slammed The First Descendant

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1026)—Answers for Thursday, April 2
item.content:encoded:
It’s time for yet another NYT Connections sweep, and you’ll be happy to know that I’m back with the best tips and solutions you can find anywhere online. If you’re eager to solve today’s NYT Connections puzzle as quickly as possible, you’ll want to scroll down and find the answers you need to do just that.

It’s almost the end of another week, but hopefully, today won’t mark the end of your NYT Connections streak!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (April 2)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • STALLION
  • CHAMPION
  • SECOND
  • CHANCE
  • MIGOS
  • MOMENT
  • BACK
  • CARDI
  • DRAKE
  • EATER
  • OPENING
  • ENDORSE
  • WINDOW
  • BUCK
  • MESON
  • DRONE

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Words you’d associate with supporting something
  • Green – You might seize these as opportunities
  • Blue – Masculine names for animals
  • Purple – The latter half of classic alcohol brands

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, April 2:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, April 2 (#1026)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – BACK, CHAMPION, ENDORSE, SECOND
  • Green – CHANCE, MOMENT, OPENING, WINDOW
  • Blue – BUCK, DRAKE, DRONE, STALLION
  • Purple – CARDI, EATER, MESON, MIGOS

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server

item.title: Capcom Jokes About Mega Man In Pragmata With This Impressive Outfit
item.content:encoded:
"April Fools!"

Yesterday, after years of speculation about Pragmata being a secret Mega Man game, Capcom finally linked the two in an April Fool's joke. As you may have already seen, it shows the game's protagonist Hugh dressed up as the Blue Bomber.

So, is this new outfit on display just a cruel joke, or is it actually going to be in the game? Fans are still debating this, but given the fact this title will have all sorts of costumes for Hugh and his android companion Diana, it seems like there's actually a serious chance of this costume being included. And just look at the detail on it, surely Capcom didn't go to all that effort for nothing!

Of course, Capcom hasn't actually said anything yet, so it remains an "April Fools" for now. And we imagine if it doesn't officially follow up on this, the community will likely step in and finish the job with a mod.

If we do end up seeing this outfit in Pragmata, it wouldn't be the first time something like this has played out in the video game space. In previous years, teams such as the Bayonetta developer Platinum transformed their own jokes into actual games.

Capcom has also been working overtime to get Mega Man back in the spotlight in recent months ahead of the release of Mega Man: Dual Override in 2027. It most recently released the Legacy Collection, and the legendary mascot has also been added to Sonic Racing: CrossWorlds as a playable DLC racer.

Pragmata is out for Switch 2 later this month on 17th April 2026, and if we hear anything else related to Mega Man, we'll let you know.

Would you like to see this costume as an unlock in Pragmata? Let us know.

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: Kyoto Xanadu details more characters, battle system
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • PS5
  • Switch
  • Switch 2

Meet Lear, Eliza, Hosen, and Luana.

Falcom has released new information and screenshots for action RPG Kyoto Xanadu introducing characters Lear Beresford, Eliza Alcott, Hosen Suruga, and Luana Calderone, as well as the game’s battle system.

Get the details below.

■ Characters

Lear Beresford (voiced by Rina Hidaka)

  • Age: 13
  • Soul Device: Floating magical tool types

The leader of the high-ranking team “Starry Night Travelers” and chairperson of the disciplinary committee, who entered Hirasaka Academy by skipping grades.

An agent dispatched by the magic society “Nemesis,” she hails from the prestigious Beresford family of England, which has produced “Chosen Ones” for generations.

She often offers opinions so grounded in reality that one would hardly believe she is only 13 years old, though she occasionally shows a more girlish side befitting her age.

Eliza Alcott (voiced by Nanako Mori)

  • Age: 18
  • Soul Device: Rapier types

A beauty in men’s clothing who serves as the assistant to Lear, leader of team “Starry Night Travelers,” as well as a member of the academy’s disciplinary committee.

With her elegant demeanor and gallant, butler-like presence, she has gained popularity among the female students.

Having originally served the Beresford family, she was dispatched to Hirasaka Academy alongside Lear.

Hosen Suruga (voiced by Nobuhiko Okamoto)

  • Age: 18
  • Soul Device: ???

The leader of the high-ranking team “Abyssal Investigators” and supervisor of Hirasaka Academy’s research facilities.

A member of the Suruga Group—one of the families that make up the international syndicate known as “Zodiac”—he serves on the student council this year as Zodiac’s representative.

He prioritizes his research above all else and has an extreme aversion to wasting time, leading him to resent even participating in the student council.

Luana Calderone (voiced by Yukiyo Fujii)

  • Age: 18
  • Soul Device: ???

A third-year student who serves as the leader of the high-ranking team “Nameless Fruit” and the head of the academy chapel.

A person overflowing with compassion and kindness, she actively participates in local volunteer work.

She is adored by many students, and some of her more fervent admirers even refer to her as the “Saint of Siena.”

■ Battle System

Kyoto Xanadu features a “Dual Dimensional” battle system that allows players to switch between 2D and 3D action. By utilizing special equipment known as “Soul Devices,” players can perform a wide variety of actions.

Standard Attack

  • A basic physical attack performed with your Soul Device.
  • Repeated button presses allow for a combo of up to three consecutive strikes.
  • Performing attacks in mid-air will trigger unique aerial standard attacks.

Strong Attack

  • A character-specific physical attack executed with your Soul Device.
  • By combining it with standard attacks, you can trigger extended attacks with effects such as increased damage against airborne enemies or area-of-effect knockback.

Dodge

  • Performs a forward dodge.
  • Dodging grants a brief period of invincibility, and allows you to slip behind certain enemies.
  • By acquiring abilities, you can unlock additional actions such as mid-air dodges and dodge attacks.

Jump

  • Performs a jump whose height varies depending on how long the button is held.
  • By acquiring abilities, you can unlock various extended actions such as recovery from knockback, wall jumps, and double jumps.

Shooting Attacks

  • Consumes the Shoot Gauge to fire a “Spirit Bullet.”
  • In 2D action, the firing direction changes based on the angle of the analog stick.
  • In 3D action, shots are fired toward the on-screen reticle.

Special Bullets

  • By acquiring abilities, you can use shooting attacks that differ from standard shots.
  • Special bullets vary by attribute, including homing shots (Fire), bombs (Wind), and laser-type attacks (Ice).

Guard

  • Hold the button to assume a defensive stance. While guarding, movement is restricted, but incoming damage is reduced.
  • Guarding at the exact moment an enemy attack lands will trigger a parry, completely nullifying the damage and significantly staggering the enemy.

Soul Accel

  • When the “Accel Gauge,” which fills as you deal or receive damage, reaches maximum, you can press the corresponding button to activate Soul Accel.
  • During Soul Accel, strong attacks are enhanced and parrying becomes easier, making it simpler to follow up with an “Issen.”

Issen

  • Successfully parrying a red, glowing warning attack will cause the Issen prompt to appear on-screen.
  • Pressing the corresponding button at the right moment triggers an Issen, which is a powerful counterattack that deals significant damage. Issen damage is further increased while Soul Accel is active.

Secret Arts

  • When the “Secret Arts Gauge,” which fills as you attack enemies, reaches maximum, you can unleash a powerful attack that damages all enemies on the battlefield. (This special attack can only be used during 3D action.)

Kyoto Xanadu is due out for PlayStation 5, Switch 2, Switch, and PC via Steam on July 16 in Japan and Asia. A western release is also planned.

View the screenshots at the gallery.

Screenshots

  • ❯

item.title: Chill with You: Lo-Fi Story coming to iOS, Android on April 8
item.content:encoded:
• Smartphone

Sound novel currently available for PC.

Nestopi will release Chill with You: Lo-Fi Story for iOS via App Store and Android via Google Play on April 8, the developer announced. It is a sound novel where you work alongside Satone, a girl who loves writing stories, and develop a special connection with her.

Chill with You: Lo-Fi Story first launched for PC via Steam on November 17, 2025.

Here is an overview of the game, via Nestopi:

About

Chill with You: Lo-Fi Story is a work-focused sound novel game where you work at your desk alongside Satone, an imaginative girl who loves writing novels. Customize the Lofi music, ambient sounds, and scenery that reflect Satone’s emotions to enhance your focus! The more time you spend working together, the deeper your bond will grow—perhaps you’ll even connect with Satone on a deeper level…?

Key Features

  • Emotionally Expressive Music to Maximize Focus – Whether you want to relax and work at a leisurely pace or boost your motivation with emotionally engaging melodies, *Chill Talk* offers a variety of original songs tailored for different work scenarios. Immerse yourself in deep focus while listening to high-quality music from talented sound artists.
  • A Wide Variety of Nature Sounds to Awaken Your Instincts – Sometimes, it’s best to turn off the music and listen to the soothing sounds of nature. Let the sound of wind, rain, and other natural elements sharpen your senses and create a deeply immersive work environment. Customize the ambient sounds to suit your preferences.
  • Pomodoro Timer for Maximum Productivity – Set up a Pomodoro timer before you start working to manage your break times effectively. Taking short, regular breaks helps maintain maximum concentration and efficiency.
  • Satone’s Hidden Feelings – During your break, take time to interact with Satone. The “precious moments” you share while working together will deepen your relationship, and if you build enough trust, she may eventually open her heart to you.

item.title: Skautfold: Moonless Knight coming to PS5, Xbox Series, PS4, Xbox One, and Switch on April 9
item.content:encoded:
Publisher Red Art Games and developer Pugware will release Metroidvania action RPG Skautfold: Moonless Knight for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series, PlayStation 4, Xbox One, and Switch on April 9 for $14.99, the companies announced.

Skautfold: Moonless Knight first launched in Early Access for PC via Steam on December 28, 2019, followed by a full release on March 4, 2020.

Here is an overview of the game, via Red Art Games:

A Skautfold Story

Return to the Lovecraftian nightmare of the Angelic Empire of Britannia as Gray, the 2nd Knight, sent on a diplomatic mission to the Dawn Empire of Japan. The mission immediately goes horribly wrong as Lunatic cultists instigate a religious coup, putting the Japanese Emperor and Eleanor’s envoys at risk. Your task? Explore the Oda Imperial Palace, negotiate with its factions, and secure the survival of the Empire!

The story follows the events of Shrouded in Sanity, Usurper, and Into the Fray, as the Skautfold series is heading to its conclusion. Knowledge of the previous games is not required to enjoy the game, but it will most certainly make it even more enjoyable.

A Tight-Knit Metroidvania

Pick your path and your battles. The world’s semi-open design allows you to forge your own way to your objective, learning shortcuts and loops as you piece together the dark secrets of the Imperial Family and its first God Emperor.

Take on bosses in any order you please. Safe heavens, familiars and Relics will allow you to grow your power and return to past areas. Along the way, tend to dying soldiers, collect Yth stones, discover ninja pugs hidden in plain sight, and dart around the Eldritch horrors posing as innocent treasures!

Explosive Combat

Fight for your life with an overhauled Guard System featuring perfect blocks and well-timed dodges. Careless actions will snowball as health damage affects your maximum guard, but it goes both ways: punish your enemies or be punished yourself!

Waltham Industry’s Vitae Injections will allow you to recover or even overcharge your health, buffing your damage and allowing you to tank troublesome attacks.

When everything goes awry and the Moon looms above, will you save the Empire or fall to Eldritch cultists?



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/skautfold-moonless-knight-coming-to-ps5-xbox-series-ps4-xbox-one-and-switch-on-april-9

item.title: Utawarerumono: Past and Present Rediscovered details more characters, field and battle systems
item.content:encoded:
■ Characters

Shunrai (voiced by Yuuki Kuwahara)

Mother of Raiko and Mirai, as well as Mikazuchi (by adoption).

She looks so young, you’d never guess she had grownup children.

Raiko (voiced by Ryoutaro Okiayu)

A Pillar General also known as Raiko the Sage.

He is Mikazuchi’s older brother, although the two of them are very different.

Raiko is a coolheaded strategist who always tries to see the bigger picture.

Ashta (voiced by Miho Okasaki)

A young girl from Arva Shulan.

She’s shorter than her younger sister, but is the more rational of the two.

Ashta and Nava are currently in Yamato on a secret mission from the Shantuura.

Nava (voiced by Maki Hanagiri)

A young girl from Arva Shulan.

She’s taller than her older sister, but is the more childlike of the two.

Ashta and Nava are currently in Yamato on a secret mission from the Shantuura.

■ System

● In the Field

Symbol Encounters

While in the field, you’ll see monsters and other enemies prowling around. In the previous game, just performing a field attack would allow you to start the battle at an advantage. In this title, you’ll need to strike the enemy from behind for this to work.

Woptors

Riding a woptor will allow you to travel a lot faster than on foot. Once you’ve met certain conditions, you can even defeat monsters instantly while riding.

Fast Travel

In the previous game, fast travel could only be used at Recollection Shrines around the country. This time around, you can fast travel any time, anywhere. Whether you’re out exploring the overworld or deep inside a dungeon, you can instantly revisit places you’ve been before.

Area Conquest

The field is divided up into different areas, which can be conquered by defeating a certain number of enemies within them. You may also have to fight powerful enemies known as Area Rulers to complete the conquest.

Once an area is conquered, you can quick kill enemies with a field attack. Enemies defeated this way will yield the same experience and rewards as if you’d fought them normally, making it a great way to level up or collect resources faster.

● Battle

Action Ring

The Action Ring in the upper left of the battle screen shows the upcoming turn order. Characters travel clockwise around the ring, taking their turn when they reach a certain point.

How fast a character moves around the ring is determined by their Speed stat. Rings closer to the center are smaller, so characters on these rings will also get their turn sooner.

A character can move to a smaller ring by inflicting Morale Loss on an enemy or by using the Move Inward command while in Overzeal.

Halu

Halu’s not just Shunya’s caretaker—he can also fight! Summon him to switch him out with the rest of the party.

Halu’s Shield (his equivalent of HP) and EP (needed to perform actions) will be displayed in the upper right of the screen. Halu is a hardy fighter, and even if his Shield reaches zero, the battle can still continue. However, if his Shield is destroyed, he’ll need time to cool off and can’t be summoned until it’s completely restored.

Halu can be upgraded at the Merchant. You can raise his stats as well as teach him new skills or improve the ones he already has.

ABL

Halu has a whole host of convenient features to take advantage of (some have been changed since the last title). To use these features, you’ll have to meet certain conditions and use the right amount of Photon Capsules.

—Example: Dress Up – Effect: Allows you to equip one additional accessory. Capsules Needed: 2.

Halu’s ATF Program

Halu now has access to ATF, or Armored Trance Form.

His attributes in this form change depending on the body type you use. Will you play it safe with the all-rounder, or focus on fast attacks to overwhelm your enemy? Experiment and pick whatever’s right for the situation!

Utawarerumono: Past and Present Rediscovered is due out for PlayStation 5 and Switch 2 in Japan and PC via Steam worldwide on May 28.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/utawarerumono-past-and-present-rediscovered-details-more-characters-field-and-battle-systems

item.title: Nintendo Really Wants You To Press Switch 2's GameChat Button
item.content:encoded:
Press the C Button to unlock your NSO reward

We're not joking about this, but just days after Nintendo ended its GameChat "Welcome Offer" promo, it's now running a new "Missions & Rewards" event, encouraging Switch 2 users to make use of the system's voice chat and video features.

This "limited time" offer allows you to unlock GameChat themed user icons when you open GameChat and complete the setup process. To get started, you need to press the C Button on your right Joy-Con 2 controller.

Of course, you'll now need to pay to access this feature. Here's the official description, along with a look:

"You can start GameChat by tapping the icon on the HOME Menu on your Nintendo Switch 2 system or by pressing the C Button on your Joy-Con™ 2 (R) controller. If you haven’t completed the initial setup for GameChat yet, you’ll need to do so before you can get your reward."

When you've fulfilled the requirements, you'll then be able to purchase these icons for 10 Platinum Points each. These icons will be available until 22nd April 2026. During this period, Switch Online members who follow these steps will also earn 100 My Nintendo Points.

In case you did miss it, Nintendo was previously running a promotion for Switch 2 owners, where they could test out all the GameChat features for free. Now that this promotion has ended, you'll need to have an active Switch Online membership to access these features.

Have you tried out GameChat on the Switch 2 yet? Let us know in the comments.

[source nintendo.com]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: Super Mario Galaxy Movie’s Charlie Day Ranks Luigi Mangione As His 2nd Favorite Luigi
item.content:encoded:
The cast of The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is currently flying around the world and doing interviews to promote the animated film, which is out now in theaters. And when Luigi’s voice actor, Charlie Day, was asked who his favorite Luigi was in “recent American history,” the Always Sunny In Philadelphia actor laughed before naming alleged murderer Luigi Mangione.

During a recent interview event in Tokyo, Japan, some of the cast of The Super Mario Galaxy Movie were chatting with an interviewer about the upcoming film. Pretty standard stuff. These interviews are usually boring fluff where guests answer pre-screened questions with media-trained responses and company-approved jokes. I imagine that for a Nintendo movie, these events are even more controlled and planned than usual. That makes Charlie Day’s answer when asked to name his favorite Luigi all the more surprising and hilarious.

❗During Super Mario Galaxy movie cast interview, actor Charlie Day (who plays Luigi) said that Luigi Mangione is one of his favorite Luigis in the recent American history. pic.twitter.com/6MwVQ1gdnU

— Luigi Archive (@LuigiArchive_) April 1, 2026

“You play Luigi, a great Italian guy,” asks the interviewer. “Who’s your favorite Luigi in recent American history?” Right after being asked, Day repeats, “In recent American history?!” while Keegan-Michael Key shouts. Then Day laughs extremely hard, and Princess Peach actress Anna Taylor-Joy hides her face with her hand.

“Well, me, first of all,” says Day, holding back laughter. “Number one. Uh, Luigi Mangione…Luigi Mangione number two.” The interviewer, as everyone laughs more, claims that’s what they wanted. Credit to that guy for offering Day the perfect chance to give that answer.

For those out of the loop, Mangione is currently facing charges for allegedly killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in New York City on December 4, 2024. That kicked off a short manhunt for Mangione, who was eventually arrested a few days later.

Since then, Mangione has become quite popular on the internet due to his good looks and many people’s hatred of insurance companies. In the aftermath of the murder, Luigi fans connected the alleged assassin to the Nintendo character, with some fans even showing up to cheer on the young man wearing Nintendo-inspired outfits. I have to imagine Nintendo hated all of that, and I bet they aren’t going to be happy about one of the movie’s main stars directly connecting Mangione to Luigi like this. What I’d give to be a fly on the wall inside Nintendo HQ right now.



https://kotaku.com/super-mario-galaxy-movies-charlie-day-ranks-luigi-mangione-as-his-2nd-favorite-luigi-2000684046

item.title: The Super Mario Galaxy Movie Is Already Breaking Box Office Records
item.content:encoded:
Wahoo!

The Super Mario Galaxy Movie is out now in cinemas, and as expected, it's already breaking box office records.

Deadline reports the film has experienced the "best opening day so far this year" in the US - reaching $34 million. This puts it ahead of Project Hail Mary on $33.1 million. Galaxy has also managed to claim the best "opening Wednesday" in April, taking the title from The Super Mario Bros. Movie, which made $31.7 million.

The same outlet previously reported that Mario's movie sequel is projected to make $350 million worldwide across the five-day Easter weekend, which would be slightly lower than the $377 million achieved by The Super Mario Bros. Movie in 2023.

Based on the trajectory of the first movie, if the "3- and 5-day numbers hold", Galaxy would bank "the biggest 5-day domestic opening" since Moana 2 ($225.4 million) and the "biggest 3-day opening" since Wicked: For Good ($147 million).

And although it's early days, Mario's new movie is already expected to "rep the biggest U.S./Canada opening" year-to-date.

The Super Mario Galaxy Movie has been labelled one "for the fans". On Rotten Tomatoes it's currently got an audience score of 92% and a critic score of 43% overall. In comparison, the audience score of the first movie was 95%, with the critic score for this one averaging out at 59%.

Here on Nintendo Life, we said Mario's Galaxy outing was a "faithful but overstuffed sequel" awarding it six out of ten stars.

Stay tuned over the coming days, weeks and months as more is revealed about The Super Mario Galaxy Movie's worldwide box office performance.

Have you seen the Super Mario Galaxy Movie yet? Let us know in the comments.

[source deadline.com]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

item.title: Petit Planet ‘Stardrift Test’ begins April 21
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Smartphone

Sign-ups now available.

miHoYo will host the “Stardrift Test” for cosmic life simulation game Petit Planet beginning April 21 across PC, iOS, and Android, the company announced. An end date was not announced. Sign-ups are now available via the official website.

The test will support the following languages: English, Japanese, French, Russian, Thai, Indonesian, and Simplified Chinese text, and English, Japanese, and Chinese voice-overs.

  • ❮
  • ❯

Here is an overview of the test, via miHoYo:

Named the “Stardrift Test,” this upcoming beta will begin on April 21, introducing new Neighbors, enhanced Starsea Voyage experiences, and additional activities at the Galactic Bazaar. The game blends the joy of creation, companionship, and community-driven experiences, transforming the universe into a canvas of infinite possibilities. Sign-ups for the closed beta are now open on the official website.

In Petit Planet, players take on the role of a Planet Tender, stepping into the vast Starsea with a planet to call their own. As planets connect to form galaxies, new stories begin to unfold. Here, you can live however you like: farming, fishing, beachcombing, cooking… Spend relaxed and fulfilling days on your planet alongside your fuzzy Neighbors, and let your imagination run wild as you decorate your home or shape your own stylish landscapes. You can also explore the Starsea and forge connections with people and things at the ever-bustling Galactic Bazaar. Each bond you form with Neighbors and the world around you weaves into an ever-growing web of connections that keeps the universe flourishing. As these bonds deepen and planets thrive, Luca, the vitality that flows through all things, shapes new landscapes and brings wondrous changes to your world.

New Neighbors will make their debut in this closed beta, each bringing their own personalities, stories, and dreams to Petit Planet. As Planet Tenders, players can invite their fuzzy companions to settle on their planet, where heartfelt conversations, thoughtful gifts, and all kinds of meaningful interactions become part of everyday life. Life on Petit Planet is further enriched with updates to the Starsea Voyage and expanded activities at the Galactic Bazaar. With the Starsea Map, players can hop on their vehicles with Neighbors and set off to explore a variety of vibrant Islets. Beyond exploration, the Galactic Bazaar remains a lively social hub, where players can gather for coffee, relax by the bonfire, try fortune-telling, dance with friends, and even take part in auctions, creating wonderful memories together.

Confirmed for PC and mobile, with additional platforms in development, Petit Planet continues to explore its vision for multiplayer gameplay and immersive cozy experiences.

Watch a new trailer below. View a new set of screenshots at the gallery.

Stardrift Test Trailer

Japanese

item.title: Cult raising simulation game TOMAK: Save the Earth Regeneration now available for PC
item.content:encoded:
• PC

Free to claim via Epic Games Store until April 16.

Publisher Netmarble and developer Netmarble Monster have released TOMAK: Save the Earth Regeneration, a remastered version of the cult hit visual novel TOMAK: Save the Earth in celebration of its 25th anniversary, for PC via Epic Games Store. It is available to claim for free until April 16, after which it will cost $8.99. It supports English, Japanese, and Korean language options.

Here is an overview of the game, via Epic Games Store:

About

To celebrate its 25th anniversary, the legendary 2001 PC classic TOMAK: Save the Earth is back, now on the Epic Games Store!

Prepare for the most bizarre yet beautiful cohabitation you’ve ever experienced. Enraged by human corruption, the gods have decreed Earth’s destruction. But Evian, the Goddess of Love, descends to save the humanity—only to lose her body due to divine opposition. Now, she’s ended up in your room… planted in a flowerpot with only her head.

You have exactly three years. Nurture her, prove that true love still exists, and help Evian regain her goddess form to save the world!

Key Features

  • A Surreal Blend of Shocking Visuals and Emotion – Experience the unconventional—a goddess in a flowerpot. Despite the bizarre concept, stunning high-detail illustrations and a lyrical soundtrack create a uniquely immersive atmosphere.
  • Deep and Authentic Life-Sim Mechanics – Evian’s survival depends entirely on your care. Engage in heartfelt conversations, build an emotional bond, and manage her growth. Your dedication over three years determines whether she ascends or the world ends.
  • Every Choice Matters – Your path is shaped by the decisions you make. From divine resurrection to tragic destruction, discover a wide range of endings based on how you raise her.

Watch the announcement trailer below.

Announce Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/cult-raising-simulation-game-tomak-save-the-earth-regeneration-now-available-for-pc

item.title: Fruit Mountain Party launches April 23
item.content:encoded:
• PC
  • Switch
  • Switch 2

Currently in Early Access for PC.

Multiplayer party 3D puzzle game Fruit Mountain Party will leave Early Access and launch for Switch 2, Switch, and PC via Steam on April 23, developer BeXide announced. Users who purchase the Switch version can upgraded to the Nintendo Switch 2 Edition via a paid “Upgrade Pack.”

Fruit Mountain Party first launched in Early Access for PC on August 28, 2025.

“During Early Access, we received a great deal of valuable feedback from players, particularly around online play,” BeXide said in the announcement. “We would like to extend our sincere thanks to everyone who supported the game during this period. For the full release, we’ve focused on improving overall stability and gameplay experience based on your feedback.”

Here is an overview of the game, via BeXide:

About

The mega-hit 3D puzzle game Fruit Mountain—where you merge fruits to make them bigger—is now a party battle game for everyone!

This time, it’s a fruit battle!? Jump into versus mode with up to four players!

With items and wild surprises, it’s a chaotic battle full of laughs!

Keep stacking those fruits!

Easy to Play

  • Just toss fruits onto the plate and stack them up.
  • Merge two of the same fruit to create a bigger one!
  • The bigger the fruit you make, the higher your score climbs!
  • Be careful not to let fruits fall off the plate—merge them skillfully!
  • Master your stacking technique and take on your rivals!

Battle Together!

  • Compete Against Rivals Worldwide Online – Create a Room and invite friends to battle anytime, anywhere!
  • Local Versus Play Offline Too – Up to four players can play locally on a single Nintendo Switch! Get everyone together for some fun!

Tons of Game Modes

Multiple versus modes! Try different styles and have a blast!

  • Standard Race
  • Thief Frenzy
  • Fruit of the Sun
  • Great Harvest
  • Survival

Single-Player Too

Hone your skills against NPCs and prepare for online battles!

In “Time Attack” mode, race to complete a watermelon as fast as you can!

Aim for the fastest time! Climb the rankings!

Collection

Customize your plate with the dress-up feature! Show off your style with your favourite plate!

Nintendo Switch 2 Edition Features

  • Frame Rate – Improved frame rates provide smoother gameplay.
  • GameShare – With just one copy of the software, you can use “GameShare” with nearby people or “GameChat” members online, allowing up to four players to play together on their own screens.

Watch a new set of trailers below.

Switch 2 Release Date Trailer

Switch Release Date Trailer



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/fruit-mountain-party-launches-april-23

item.title: Nagi no Koi for PS4, Switch launches April 23
item.content:encoded:
• PS4
  • Switch

Features both English and Japanese language options.

The PlayStation 4 and Switch versions of Japanese-style visual novel Nagi no Koi will launch digitally on April 23 for 2,700 yen, publisher Entergram and developer Nutrients announced. It will support both English and Japanese language options.

Nagi no Koi first launched for PC on August 20, 2021 in Japan, followed by an English-localized Steam release on January 14, 2025.

While the Steam version only included voice-overs for the four female leads, the console versions add voices to the male and minor female characters for a fully voiced experience, as well as feature a revamped user interface.

Here is an overview of the game, via its Steam page:

About

Nagi no Koi is a Japanese-style visual novel where two stories intersect.

Players read through the story and make choices at branching points to change it.

Key Features

  • Genre: Japanese visual novel where players made choices based on their feelings.
  • Play time: Six hours (if you listen to all of the voice lines).
  • Overview: The game is divided in two parts. (The first part is divided into two chapters, each of which consisting of three parts following a traditional Japanese narrative structure: introduction, climax, and closure. After completing the two chapters players can then choose the second part of the story.)
  • Voices: Only the main female characters are voiced.

Recommended For

  • People who like visual novel type games.
  • People who like emotionally intense stories.
    People who like pure love stories.

Synopsis

—Chapter of the Red Spider Lily

Masahide’s dream was to become the Lord that would rule this land.

But his dream, along with his ego, was crushed, leaving him without any pride.

It was then where a girl named Sahana scolded him for living his life as a mere cog of the government.

He enters into a loveless political marriage with Sahana, but that relationship slowly changes them both.

As it gradually transforms into a passionate relationship, Masahide decides to pursue a dream he once gave up on.

—Chapter of the Water Lily

A man without memories.

Completely lost, unable to remember even his name, is saved by a girl named Suirin.

For Suirin’s sake, he turns his existence around.

Across the sea, where the nagidori calls.

Kai’s memories lie dormant in the sea.

Cast

  • Suirin (voiced by Tanemaki)
  • Sahana (voiced by Yurara)
  • Shenhua (voiced by Sasorihime)
  • Aoi (voiced by Sakura)

Mature Content in This Game

This game contains depictions of violence, gore, references to sexual acts, and scenes involving alcohol consumption.



https://www.gematsu.com/2026/04/nagi-no-koi-for-ps4-switch-launches-april-23

item.title: 'The Hundred Line' Update 1.2.0 Announced, Here's What's Included
item.content:encoded:
Improvements, bug fixes and more

Too Kyo Games and Marvelous are still showing support for The Hundred Line - Last Defense Academy in 2026 with regular updates, and the latest one has now been announced.

It's a relatively small one compared to some of the previous updates, but it does include some notable improvements such as added language support and colorblind support via the options menu. As usual, there are also some bug fixes to enhance the overall experience.

Here's what you can expect, according to the official Steam game page:

The Hundred Line - Last Defense Academy (Version 1.2.0)

[ver1.2.0] Update Notice 2026/4/1

Improvements

- Changed the ability to start a new game from "HUNDRED LINE 2"

- Changed the ability to skip enemy movement animations during battles using the cancel button

- Changed the ability to select from the beginning of Day 0 in Chapter Select

- Added "Colorblind Support" to the options

- Added Korean language support (text)

Bug Fixes

- Fixed a bug where some achievements were not unlocking

- Fixed other minor bugs and typos

Thank you for your continued support of ”The Hundred Line -Last Defense Academy-”.

This follows Too Kyo Games and Marvelous revealing earlier this year that this title is actually two games in one. In our review here on Nintendo Life last April, we gave this game an "excellent" nine out ten stars.

Have you tried out this game on the Switch yet? How are you finding it so far? Let us know in the comments.

[source store.steampowered.com]

Liam is a news writer and reviewer on Nintendo Life. He's been writing about games for more than 15 years and is a lifelong fan of many iconic video game characters.

  • Reply
